summary.htmlContent:
Quality Council Meeting Summary

Top 3 Problems Identified

  • High number of overdue effectiveness checks and long-term actions.
  • Repeated deviations due to human factors and knowledge gaps in processes.
  • Resource constraints impacting commissioning of M building and other critical activities.

Decisions Made

  • Keep high number of overdue effectiveness checks as a priority for monitoring.
  • Escalate TI-related issues to higher management for alternative solutions.
  • Proceed with planning for periodic reviews and formalizing knowledge sessions.
  • Maintain focus on reducing TI count for M2 and improving compliance.

Escalations Required

  • Escalate need for long-term solution for temporary instructions to central quality.
  • Escalate resource constraints for M building commissioning if unresolved by January.
  • Escalate TI process improvement to Psychology Council for alternative solutions.

Communications to cascade to team

  • Emphasize importance of timely closure of long-term actions and effectiveness checks.
  • Share updates on QIP actions and expectations for 2026.
  • Communicate plans for knowledge sessions and training optimization.
  • Inform about escalations and resource planning for M building commissioning.
